source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
source.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
source.setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.13 — sensory symptoms including tingling or numbness in adults

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

items.final_wording: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Low Back Pain – 15
source.population: Adults presenting with acute low back pain.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for acute adult low-back-pain telehealth documentation in Dubai; partial for chronic pain, in-person examination, and settings outside Dubai.

exact_location.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability; 5–6 Virtual assessment and clinical history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.

items.rationale: Supports Dubai virtual low-back and radiating-symptom history for sciatica review; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.
